_comment: bugsweep configuration. Empty command strings mean auto-detect. Edit caps to bound cost/runtime on unattended runs.

adversarial._comment: bugsweep-hcj: referee_votes (K) is the number of independent Referee adjudications (same rubric, varied framing) required for a severity >= high/critical finding to become fix-eligible -- it needs a STRICT MAJORITY of CONFIRMED verdicts (see prompts/referee.md and bench/scorer/run_summary.py's majority_gate). referee_votes_cap is a hard ceiling on K so a misconfigured value can't blow up adjudication cost on an unattended run -- effective K = min(referee_votes, referee_votes_cap). Severity below high (medium/low) is unaffected: single-pass adjudication, unchanged.

context._comment: Coverage-first cross-run scope. State lives in .bugsweep/state/ (gitignored). The hunt always spans the whole repo; these only reprioritize the queue.

priority._comment: Local-only, deterministic why-now evidence. Signals reorder investigation but never confirm a finding, relax fix gates, or narrow whole-repo scope. Fresh, provenanced project snapshots may be written to .bugsweep/priority-signals.jsonl; Bugsweep never calls their remote services itself. Signal outcomes are observed for yield reporting only and never mutate weights automatically.

recall._comment: bugsweep-dxh: --recall mode. Lowers the bar ONLY for what gets reported — the Referee additionally records near-misses (confidence 50-67: genuinely plausible but not >67% CONFIRMED) to run-summary.json's near_misses[] for human review. It NEVER lowers the auto-fix threshold: near-misses are never promoted into fixed/quarantined/confirmed_unfixed/findings (enforced in bench/scorer/run_summary.py, independent of this config value). Overridable per-invocation via 'scripts/summarize.sh <RUN_DIR> <REPORT_IS_STUB> [MODE] --recall'.

analyzers._comment: Optional pre-hunt step (scripts/analyzers.sh): runs off-the-shelf static analyzers (semgrep, gosec, bandit, ...) to seed the Hunter with candidate locations and give the Referee a corroboration signal. Defaults to disabled — a reproducibility default: bugsweep's own findings are the audited, adversarially-reviewed output; auto-running third-party tools changes run behavior based on what happens to be installed on the machine, which would make two runs of the identical repo diverge. Opt in explicitly once you've reviewed what's installed.

verify._comment: flaky_reruns: how many times run_checks.sh verify reruns a newly-failing 'test' check. It is classified flaky (and excluded from the revert) ONLY on a strict majority of rerun passes; a tie or a majority of rerun failures still reverts. Reruns share the working tree/environment, so this is not full isolation — see scripts/run_checks.sh header for the accurate safety claim, the state-pollution caveat, and the baseline-flaky limitation.
